content: {"perspective": "Industry", "question": "Round 1 identified the JMIR 52-week observational study of 2 IF apps as the main real-world evidence source and reported 1% weight loss per eligible fasting hour per day by week 26. What were the specific app names studied, the sample size, the week-by-week retention curve, the breakdown of fasting protocols used by engaged users (16:8 vs 18:6 vs 5:2 vs ADF), the correlation between self-reported weight loss and eligible fasting hours, and whether the study reported any cardiovascular, safety, or adverse event data that would speak to the RCT-literature safety concerns raised by the 2024 AHA mortality signal?", "search_queries": ["JMIR 52-week observational study intermittent fasting app retention weight loss Zero LIFE sample", "intermittent fasting app 52 weeks eligible fasting hours weight loss correlation protocol breakdown", "Zero LIFE Fasting Tracker app observational study retention curve protocol 16:8 5:2 ADF", "intermittent fasting app adverse events self-reported safety real-world observational", "Torres Mendez 2022 Zero LIFE app JMIR 52-week fasting retention weight loss mHealth"]}
